_meta.description: Phase 2 task list: hardening the disassembly for randomizer, editor, and modding tooling. All Phase 1 cleanup (139 tasks) is complete.

acceptance: No hex-to-decimal conversions in dc.b/dc.w/dc.l tables
New labels follow repo conventions; loc_ renames preserve original address in a comment
New constants go in the appropriate split constants file (hw_constants, game_constants, sound_constants, or ram_addresses)
editing_rules: Prefer symbolic constants for RAM and I/O addresses
Prefer existing macros over repeated instruction sequences
Avoid moving code across modules unless callers are updated and verify.bat remains bit-perfect
Use Node.js for all scripting (no Python)

always: Run cmd //c verify.bat after any change
If verify fails, fix cause; do not revert unrelated user changes
Run tools/run_checks.js to verify linters pass
before_refactors: Add/confirm label-level comments explaining invariants and expected register usage
Confirm branches (BSR/BRA) remain in range or change to JSR/JMP
Confirm all dc.* tables remain byte-identical (keep hex literals)

comment_density.description: Raise comment density in under-documented files. Target: 15%+ comment-only lines in code files, function headers on every exported routine.

tasks.title: Document sram.asm (1.2% comment density)
tasks.description: sram.asm has only 4 comment lines in 324 lines of code. Add function headers with input/output registers, document SRAM layout and checksum algorithm ($8810 polynomial), annotate raw loop counts ($000D, $0015, $000F, $002F, $01FF, $014E, $029F) with their semantic meanings (data block sizes), and document raw SRAM struct offsets ($2(A0), $4(A0), $6(A0)).

tasks.title: Document magic.asm (1.9% comment density)
tasks.description: magic.asm has only 27 comment lines in 1405 lines. Misleading filename: it actually contains ending credits/outro rendering, staff credit strings, and epilogue scene code. Add function headers, document the VDP tilemap rendering for credits scrolling, explain raw tile attribute values ($A080, $C180, $84C0, $4002), and annotate the ~12 ORI.l #$40000003 VDP row-advance patterns.

tasks.title: Document bossbattle.asm (3.7% comment density)
tasks.description: bossbattle.asm has only 29 comment lines in 780 lines. Contains wall tile offset tables, FP sprite positions, boss battle player initialization, and cave map code. Add function headers, document data table formats, annotate raw VDP longwords ($40AE0003, $60AE0003) and raw immediates ($0090, $85B7).

tasks.title: Document items.asm (4.6% comment density)
tasks.description: items.asm has only 153 comment lines in 3339 lines. Contains the full item use system, shop buy/sell system, and dialogue state machine. Add function headers with register conventions, document the shop flow, explain the 6 instances of ANDI.l #$00FFFFFF (ROM address masking), and annotate raw struct offsets.

tasks.title: Document dungeon.asm (8.2% comment density)
tasks.description: dungeon.asm has 186 comment lines in 2257 lines. The first-person wall rendering system is complex. Document raw map sector stride offsets (-$30(A2), $30(A2), -$60(A2), $60(A2), -$1(A2)), annotate the ~13 raw VDP longwords, and add comments to the tile decompression and wall draw functions.

tasks.title: Document cutscene.asm (9.4% comment density)
tasks.description: cutscene.asm has 203 comment lines in 2169 lines. Contains prologue scenes and town tilemap rendering. Document the ~25+ raw VDP command longwords, explain raw tile attribute bases ($A300, $C180, $84C0, $E300), annotate raw immediates ($0300, $01FF, $0E00), and document the repeated ADDI.l #$00800000 VDP row-advance pattern.

tasks.title: Document enemy.asm (9.6% comment density)
tasks.description: enemy.asm has 347 comment lines in 3608 lines. Contains enemy AI, projectile systems, collision, and Boss 1's full state machine. Boss 1 AI has many raw velocity longwords ($FFFF8000, $FFFFC000, $FFFE0000, $00020000) and raw position values ($005A, $0060, $006E, $0070, $00DA, $00C8, $00AC) that need annotation.

tasks.title: Document towndata.asm (9.9% comment density)
tasks.description: towndata.asm has 389 comment lines in 3922 lines. Starts mid-function (continuation from items.asm). Contains locked door data table (LockedDoorDataTable at line 119) as entirely raw bytes, town NPC data, cave events, and chest reward code. Document the locked door format and add per-entry annotations.

magic_numbers_phase2.description: Replace remaining magic numbers with named constants across all files. Phase 1 covered core/vblank/states/init/battle_gfx; these are the remaining files.

tasks.title: Define VDP_VRAM_ROW_STRIDE constant for $00800000 pattern
tasks.description: The pattern ADDI.l #$00800000, Dx (advance VDP write address by one nametable row) appears dozens of times across cutscene.asm, magic.asm, miscdata.asm, bossbattle.asm, dungeon.asm. Define VDP_VRAM_ROW_STRIDE equ $00800000 in hw_constants.asm and replace all instances.

tasks.title: Define ROM_ADDRESS_MASK constant for $00FFFFFF pattern
tasks.description: ANDI.l #$00FFFFFF, Dx (mask ROM address to 24-bit) appears 8+ times across items.asm, npc.asm, player.asm. Define ROM_ADDRESS_MASK equ $00FFFFFF in hw_constants.asm and replace all instances.

tasks.title: Replace ~329 raw VDP command longwords with vdpCmd macro calls
tasks.description: ~329 raw VDP command longwords (patterns like $4xxxxxxx, $6xxxxxxx, $7xxxxxxx) remain across all source files. The vdpCmd macro already exists in macros.asm. Systematically replace raw longwords with vdpCmd(addr, type) calls. Do file-by-file, verify bit-perfect after each.
tasks.note: The vdpCmd macro must produce byte-identical output. Test with one instance first, verify, then batch per file.

tasks.title: Define named constants for raw tile attribute bases in cutscene/magic
tasks.description: Tile attribute base values ($A080, $C180, $84C0, $E300, $A300, $4002) are used throughout cutscene.asm and magic.asm for tilemap writes. These encode palette, priority, and base tile index. Define named constants with comments explaining the bit decomposition.

tasks.title: Define named constants for Boss 1 AI parameters in enemy.asm
tasks.description: Boss 1 state machine uses raw velocity longwords ($FFFF8000, $FFFFC000, $FFFE0000, $00020000) and raw position bounds ($005A, $0060, $006E, $0070, $00DA, $00C8, $00AC). Define named constants like BOSS1_MOVE_SPEED, BOSS1_CHARGE_SPEED, BOSS1_ARENA_LEFT, etc.

tasks.title: Define SRAM constants for raw offsets and sizes in sram.asm
tasks.description: sram.asm uses raw loop counts ($000D, $0015, $000F, $002F, $01FF, $014E, $029F) for data block sizes and a raw checksum polynomial ($8810). Define named constants (SRAM_BLOCK_SIZE_*, SRAM_CHECKSUM_POLY, etc.) in game_constants.asm.

tasks.title: Define named constants for locked door data in towndata.asm
tasks.description: LockedDoorDataTable at towndata.asm:119 is entirely raw hex bytes. Each entry encodes town ID, door position, and required key item. Define per-field constants and add per-entry comments identifying which door each entry represents.

tasks.title: Define debug screen tile constants in miscdata.asm
tasks.description: Debug test menu in miscdata.asm uses raw tile indices ($04DE, $04DF, $04DD, $04DC, $250F, $2506, $450F, $450E, $44C0) and raw VDP write addresses ($45960003, etc.). Define named constants for the debug font tile base and the debug screen layout.

sound_driver_constants.description: The sound driver in sound.asm uses ~41 raw $00FFF* RAM addresses and raw YM2612/Z80 DAC addresses. Define symbolic constants for the entire sound driver RAM map.

tasks.title: Define sound driver RAM address constants
tasks.description: sound.asm uses ~41 raw sound RAM addresses like $00FFF430, $00FFF421, $00FFF520, $00FFF5E0 as instruction operands. The file header already documents the layout in comments. Extract these into named constants in sound_constants.asm (e.g., SndRAM_cmd_queue, SndRAM_tempo_ctr, SndRAM_fm_ch1, etc.) and replace all raw references.

tasks.title: Define YM2612 and Z80 DAC port constants
tasks.description: sound.asm uses raw YM2612 port addresses ($00A04000-$00A04003) and Z80 DAC addresses ($00A01FFF, $00A01FFE, $00A01FFD). Define named constants (YM2612_addr_port_1, YM2612_data_port_1, YM2612_addr_port_2, YM2612_data_port_2, Z80_dac_sample, Z80_dac_bank, Z80_dac_status) in hw_constants.asm.

tasks.title: Document and name sound driver timing constants
tasks.description: The sound driver uses raw timing values for tempo, note duration tables, and envelope rates. Identify all timing-related magic numbers in sound.asm and define named constants in sound_constants.asm.

macro_expansion.description: Create new macros for repeated patterns discovered in Phase 2 analysis, and expand adoption of existing macros.

tasks.title: Create VdpRowAdvance macro for ADDI.l #$00800000 pattern
tasks.description: The VDP nametable row advance pattern (ADDI.l #$00800000, Dx) appears 30+ times. After MAGICB-001 defines the constant, consider also creating a one-line macro VdpRowAdvance that expands to ADDI.l #VDP_VRAM_ROW_STRIDE, \1 for even cleaner code. Verify byte-identical expansion.

tasks.title: Create InterruptDisable / InterruptEnable macros
tasks.description: The pattern ORI #$0700, SR (disable interrupts) and ANDI #$F8FF, SR (enable interrupts) appears frequently across sound.asm, core.asm, and vblank.asm. Create InterruptDisable/InterruptEnable macros in macros.asm. Verify byte-identical expansion with both ORI/ANDI instruction encodings.
tasks.note: Must verify ASM68K generates identical bytes for the macro expansion vs inline instructions

tasks.title: Create RomAddrMask macro for ANDI.l #$00FFFFFF pattern
tasks.description: The ROM address masking pattern (ANDI.l #$00FFFFFF, Dx) appears 8+ times. After MAGICB-002 defines the constant, optionally create a RomAddrMask macro. Test byte-identical expansion.

tasks.title: Create SaveSR / RestoreSR macros for status register save/restore pattern
tasks.description: Several sound driver and DMA routines save/restore the status register with MOVE.w SR, -(SP) / MOVE.w (SP)+, SR. If this appears 5+ times, create SaveSR/RestoreSR macros.

struct_offset_cleanup.description: Replace remaining raw struct offsets with named constants. Phase 1 covered obj_* fields for A5/A6; Phase 2 covers other struct types.

tasks.title: Define SRAM save slot struct offsets
tasks.description: sram.asm uses raw offsets like $2(A0), $4(A0), $6(A0) for SRAM slot fields. Reverse-engineer the save slot struct layout, define named constants (sram_slot_name, sram_slot_level, sram_slot_hp, etc.) in game_constants.asm, and replace all raw offsets.

tasks.title: Define dungeon map stride constants
tasks.description: dungeon.asm uses raw map sector stride offsets (-$30(A2), $30(A2), -$60(A2), $60(A2), -$1(A2)) for navigating the dungeon tile grid. Define named constants (DUNGEON_ROW_STRIDE, DUNGEON_2ROW_STRIDE, etc.) that explain the grid dimensions these derive from.

tasks.title: Define item struct field offsets in items.asm
tasks.description: items.asm uses raw struct offsets for shop items and inventory entries. Audit all $NN(Ax) patterns in items.asm, define named constants for each field, and replace.

tasks.title: Define locked door entry struct offsets in towndata.asm
tasks.description: LockedDoorDataTable entries use implicit struct fields. Define constants for entry size and field offsets (lockdoor_town_id, lockdoor_x, lockdoor_y, lockdoor_key_item, etc.).

misplaced_data_annotation.description: Annotate data that is stranded in wrong modules due to bit-perfect constraints. Cannot move, but can thoroughly document.

tasks.title: Document misplaced town tileset data in sound.asm
tasks.description: sound.asm contains TownTilesetPtrs_Entry02 and TownTilesetPtrs_Entry03 (town tileset data) plus battle HUD incbins stranded among the sound driver code. Phase 1 noted the constraint (MOVE-006). Add prominent section separators and detailed comments explaining why this data is here, what it represents, and where it logically belongs.

tasks.title: Document cross-file function continuation in towndata.asm
tasks.description: towndata.asm starts mid-function, continuing code from items.asm. This is a ROM layout artifact. Add a prominent header comment explaining the cross-file continuation, what function it's part of, and where the function begins in items.asm.

tasks.title: Document ending credits code in magic.asm
tasks.description: magic.asm is misleadingly named: it primarily contains ending credits/outro rendering and staff credit strings, not magic spell code. Add a file header clarifying the actual contents, explain why it's named magic.asm (ROM layout from original binary), and document the credits rendering system.

tooling_phase2.description: New tooling to support randomizer/editor development and ongoing maintenance.

tasks.title: Create map data viewer/dumper (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/map_viewer.js that reads the ROM binary, decompresses RLE-encoded map data, and outputs ASCII/text visualizations of overworld sectors, town maps, and dungeon rooms. Essential for verifying map edits and understanding spatial layout.

tasks.title: Create palette viewer/dumper (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/palette_viewer.js that extracts and displays all palette data from the ROM in a human-readable format (RGB values, color swatches as terminal output or HTML). Supports the graphics editor workflow.

tasks.title: Create encounter rate analyzer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/encounter_analyzer.js that reads encounter tables from the ROM and outputs: encounter rates per map region, enemy group composition, expected difficulty curves. Essential for randomizer balance testing.

tasks.title: Create progression logic validator (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/progression_validator.js that reads the town/dungeon connectivity graph, story trigger requirements, and item locations to verify the game is completable. Foundation for randomizer logic validation.

tasks.title: Create ROM diff tool (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/rom_diff.js that compares two ROM binaries and reports differences by label/function name (using symbol_map.json). More useful than raw hex diff for diagnosing verify failures and reviewing patches.

tasks.title: Create lint for raw VDP command longwords
tasks.description: Write tools/lint_raw_vdp_cmds.js that scans src/*.asm for VDP command longwords not using the vdpCmd macro. Prevents regression after MAGICB-003 converts existing instances.

tasks.title: Create comment density reporter
tasks.description: Write tools/comment_density.js that reports comment-line percentage for every src/*.asm file, highlighting files below the 15% threshold. Tracks documentation progress over time.

tasks.title: Create magic number audit tool
tasks.description: Write tools/audit_magic_numbers.js that scans instruction operands for hex literals not matching any constant in *_constants.asm files. Reports file, line, and value. Replaces the Phase 1 manual audit approach (BUILD-001).

randomizer_foundation.description: Build the actual randomizer framework on top of Phase 1's documentation work.

tasks.title: Design randomizer architecture document
tasks.description: Write docs/randomizer_architecture.md: define the randomizer's approach (binary patching vs ASM reassembly), seed format, which data tables are randomized, logic validation requirements, and output format. This is the design spec before implementation.

tasks.title: Implement enemy stat randomizer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/enemy_randomizer.js that reads enemy stat data (from tools/data/enemies.json), applies configurable randomization (stat variance, element shuffle, ability shuffle), validates constraints (no impossible enemies), and writes patched data back via tools/inject_game_data.js.

tasks.title: Implement shop inventory randomizer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/shop_randomizer.js that shuffles shop inventories while respecting progression constraints (e.g., healing items available early, endgame weapons only in late towns).

tasks.title: Implement treasure chest randomizer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/chest_randomizer.js that shuffles treasure chest contents. Uses the chest catalog from RAND-008 and progression logic from TOOLB-004 to ensure all key items remain reachable.

tasks.title: Implement encounter table randomizer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/encounter_randomizer.js that shuffles enemy encounter groups by region while maintaining difficulty curve. Can swap enemy types within similar power tiers.

tasks.title: Implement player stat curve randomizer (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/stats_randomizer.js that varies the player's level-up stat gains within configurable bounds. Uses data from tools/data/player_stats.json.

tasks.title: Create unified randomizer CLI (Node.js)
tasks.description: Write tools/randomizer/randomize.js as the main entry point: accepts a seed, config flags (which systems to randomize, variance levels), runs all sub-randomizers, validates the result with progression_validator, and outputs a patched ROM.

tasks.title: Write randomizer user guide
tasks.description: Write docs/randomizer_guide.md: how to use the randomizer CLI, configuration options, seed sharing, known limitations, and FAQ.

editor_foundation.description: Build the actual game editor framework on top of Phase 1's documentation and extraction/injection tooling.

tasks.title: Design editor architecture document
tasks.description: Write docs/editor_architecture.md: define the editor's approach (web-based vs desktop, data flow between JSON and ASM, supported edit types, preview capabilities). This is the design spec before implementation.

tasks.title: Create enemy stat editor (Node.js CLI)
tasks.description: Write tools/editor/enemy_editor.js: interactive CLI that loads enemies.json, presents editable fields with validation (stat ranges, element types), and writes changes back to the ASM source via inject_game_data.js.

tasks.title: Create shop inventory editor (Node.js CLI)
tasks.description: Write tools/editor/shop_editor.js: interactive CLI for editing shop inventories, prices, and item availability. Validates item IDs against known constants.

tasks.title: Create text/dialogue editor (Node.js CLI)
tasks.description: Write tools/editor/text_editor.js: builds on script_patch.js to provide interactive dialogue editing. Shows decoded strings, allows editing, validates byte length constraints, and patches back into source.

tasks.title: Create player stat curve editor (Node.js CLI)
tasks.description: Write tools/editor/stats_editor.js: edit level-up stat gains and equipment modifiers. Shows stat progression graphs in terminal. Validates balance constraints.

tasks.title: Create map data editor (Node.js CLI)
tasks.description: Write tools/editor/map_editor.js: reads decompressed map data (from map_viewer.js), allows tile editing in a grid view, recompresses with RLE, and patches the ASM source. Must handle overworld sectors, town maps, and dungeon rooms.

dead_code_deep_audit.description: Phase 1 annotated 4 known dead code blocks. Perform a comprehensive dead code audit across all files.

tasks.title: Audit all files for unreachable code after unconditional branches
tasks.description: Scan all src/*.asm files for instructions immediately following BRA/JMP/RTS that are not reachable by any label. Flag as dead code with dc.b encoding and explanatory comments. The magic.asm orphan RTS bytes (DEAD-002) were found this way; systematically check all files.

tasks.title: Cross-reference all function labels against callsite index
tasks.description: Using tools/index/callsites.json and tools/index/functions.json, identify functions with zero external callers. These may be dead code, or may be called via computed jumps (offset tables). For each, determine if truly dead or table-dispatched, and annotate accordingly.

regression_prevention.description: Automated checks to prevent common mistakes and ensure ongoing quality.

tasks.title: Create pre-commit hook script
tasks.description: Write tools/pre-commit.sh (and tools/pre-commit.cmd for Windows) that runs: (1) tools/run_checks.js (all linters), (2) cmd //c verify.bat (bit-perfect check). Add installation instructions to docs/contributing.md. Prevents broken commits.

tasks.title: Add lint for comment density regression
tasks.description: Extend tools/run_checks.js to include the comment density reporter (TOOLB-007) as a warning (not failure) when any file drops below 10% comment density. Tracks documentation regression.

tasks.title: Add lint for struct offset constants regression
tasks.description: After STRUCT-001 through STRUCT-004 replace raw struct offsets, add a lint to tools/run_checks.js that flags any new raw $NN(Ax) patterns in modified files. Prevents re-introduction of undocumented offsets.

notes_modernization.description: Synchronize and modernize notes.txt with the current state of the codebase.

tasks.title: Update notes.txt to reference symbolic names
tasks.description: notes.txt contains developer reverse-engineering notes that reference raw hex addresses (e.g., $FFFFC400, $FFFFC62C) which now have symbolic names in ram_addresses.asm and game_constants.asm. Update notes.txt to use symbolic names alongside hex addresses for cross-referencing.

tasks.title: Remove outdated/superseded information from notes.txt
tasks.description: Some information in notes.txt has been superseded by detailed documentation now in the source files themselves (map format in mapdata.asm, object struct in constants.asm, script opcodes in script.asm). Review notes.txt and either remove superseded sections or add cross-references to the canonical locations.

test_reference.description: Manage the legacy reference file and establish any additional verification.

tasks.title: Evaluate removing vermilion_test.asm from repo
tasks.description: vermilion_test.asm (78,461 lines, the original unsplit disassembly) is kept as reference. It's 3+ MB of text in the repo. Evaluate whether git history suffices as the reference, and if so, remove the file to reduce repo size. If keeping it, compress or move to a 'reference' branch.

priority_order: --- Phase 2C: VDP command cleanup (biggest single improvement) ---
MAGICB-003 (329 raw VDP longwords -> vdpCmd macro)
--- Phase 2D: Remaining magic numbers and struct offsets ---
MAGICB-004 through MAGICB-008 (tile attrs, boss AI, SRAM, locked doors, debug screen)
STRUCT-001 through STRUCT-004 (SRAM slots, dungeon stride, item fields, locked doors)
